what: Scenario 1 — Load multiple files into R
title: Working with multiple RCDF files
text: Pass character vector file paths (folder path) read_rcdf(). Provide matching vectors keys passwords files encrypted different keys: Requirements stacking work: - RCDF file must contain tables names column structure. - Passwords must correspond positionally decryption keys.
code: data <- read_rcdf(   path = c(     "path/to/file/01.rcdf",     "path/to/file/02.rcdf",     "path/to/file/03.rcdf"   ),   decryption_key = c(     "keys/01-private-key.pem",     "keys/02-private-key.pem",     "keys/03-private-key.pem"   ),   password = c(     "password01",     "password02",     "password03"   ) )

what: Scenario 2 — Merge files into a new RCDF file
title: Working with multiple RCDF files
text: merge_rcdf() reads multiple RCDF files, stacks tables, saves result brand-new encrypted RCDF file. handy creating archive distributing combined dataset collaborators: merged file encrypted key, supply via pub_key argument (see ?merge_rcdf options). reading single files exporting data, see vignette("rcdf").
code: merge_rcdf(   rcdf_files = c(     "path/to/file/01.rcdf",     "path/to/file/02.rcdf",     "path/to/file/03.rcdf"   ),   decryption_key = c(     "keys/01-private-key.pem",     "keys/02-private-key.pem",     "keys/03-private-key.pem"   ),   password = c(     "password01",     "password02",     "password03"   ),   merged_file_path = "path/to/merged.rcdf" )

title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: Think RCDF file locked cabinet data. can store multiple datasets inside , cabinet can opened right key. share file colleague, see contents unless also give copy key. hood, RCDF file single portable archive (.rcdf) holds datasets compressed, encrypted files along small metadata record. encryption happens automatically — never configure .

title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: package ships sample RCDF file matching keys can try everything without creating files first:
code: library(rcdf) sample_dir  <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf") sample_rcdf <- file.path(sample_dir, "mtcars.rcdf") sample_key  <- file.path(sample_dir, "sample-private-key.pem")

title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: saving data, need key pair — two files work together: Generate new pair one function call: Important: private key way open files later. Back store securely. password manager encrypted drive good choice.
code: pub_key <- generate_rsa_keys(   path     = "~/my-keys",         # folder where both files are saved   password = "a-strong-password"  # optional, but protects the private key ) # pub_key holds the path to the public key — you will pass it to write_rcdf()

title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: Organise datasets named list, call write_rcdf(): creates survey-2024.rcdf — single, portable file containing tables, fully encrypted ready share.
code: # Create a container for your tables my_data <- rcdf_list()  # Add any data frames you want to store together my_data$households <- data.frame(id = 1:100, region = sample(c("North", "South"), 100, TRUE)) my_data$survey     <- data.frame(id = 1:100, score  = rnorm(100))  # Save everything as a single encrypted file write_rcdf(   data    = my_data,   path    = "~/data/survey-2024.rcdf",   pub_key = pub_key              # path returned by generate_rsa_keys() )

title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: open RCDF file, provide matching private key: Try right now using bundled sample data:
code: survey_data <- read_rcdf(   path           = "~/data/survey-2024.rcdf",   decryption_key = "~/my-keys/private-key.pem",   password       = "a-strong-password"   # only needed if you set one )  # Each table comes back as a data frame inside the list head(survey_data$households) head(survey_data$survey) sample_data <- read_rcdf(   path           = sample_rcdf,   decryption_key = file.path(sample_dir, "sample-private-key-pw.pem"),   password       = "1234" )  head(sample_data$mtcars)

what: Reading a whole folder at once
title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: many RCDF files one folder, load one call. Tables share name across files automatically stacked single table:
code: all_data <- read_rcdf(   path           = "~/data/monthly-exports/",   decryption_key = "~/my-keys/private-key.pem",   password       = "a-strong-password",   recursive      = FALSE   # set TRUE to also search sub-folders )

title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: data R, export whatever format team needs: supported formats: can also call individual functions directly (e.g. write_rcdf_csv(), write_rcdf_xlsx()) need control. Run ?write_rcdf_as see options.
code: write_rcdf_as(   data    = survey_data,   path    = "~/exports/survey-2024",   formats = c("csv", "xlsx")   # create both at once ) # Result: #   ~/exports/survey-2024/CSV/households.csv #   ~/exports/survey-2024/CSV/survey.csv #   ~/exports/survey-2024/Excel/households.xlsx #   ~/exports/survey-2024/Excel/survey.xlsx

what: Checking a file without decrypting it
title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: can inspect RCDF file created, package version wrote , integrity checksums — without private key:
code: meta <- get_attrs(sample_rcdf) meta$created_at   # when the file was created meta$version      # package version used to create it meta$checksum     # per-table checksums for integrity verification

title: Getting Started with rcdf
text: Working multiple files — see vignette("merging-rcdf") loading combining RCDF files different sources. Full function reference — run ?write_rcdf, ?read_rcdf, ?write_rcdf_as R console available options.

title: Authors and Citation

title: Authors and Citation
text: Abdulsamad B (2026). rcdf: Comprehensive Toolkit Working Encrypted Parquet Files. R package version 0.1.6, https://yng-.github.io/rcdf/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rcdf: A Comprehensive Toolkit for Working with Encrypted Parquet Files},   author = {Bhas Abdulsamad},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.6},   url = {https://yng-me.github.io/rcdf/}, }

what: A Comprehensive Toolkit for Working with Encrypted Parquet Files
title: A Comprehensive Toolkit for Working with Encrypted Parquet Files
text: rcdf package comprehensive toolkit securely working RCDF (encrypted Parquet) files R. RCDF custom data format designed provide strong encryption metadata management sensitive datasets. rcdf, users can easily handle encrypted data, including reading, writing, exporting data stored secure format. Key features include: Secure data handling: Functions reading writing encrypted Parquet files using AES RSA encryption. Metadata management: Tools handling storing metadata RCDF files, including automated key generation decryption. Parquet integration: Full integration arrow package read write Parquet files seamlessly. Cross-platform support: Compatible Linux, macOS, Windows environments.

title: A Comprehensive Toolkit for Working with Encrypted Parquet Files
text: Install CRAN : can also install development version rcdf GitHub : installed, can load package start working RCDF files.
code: install.packages("rcdf") # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("yng-me/rcdf") library(rcdf)

what: Add metadata attributes to a data frame — add_metadata
title: Add metadata attributes to a data frame — add_metadata
text: Adds variable labels value labels data frame based metadata dictionary. particularly useful preparing datasets use packages like haven exporting formats like SPSS Stata.

title: Add metadata attributes to a data frame — add_metadata
code: add_metadata(data, metadata, ..., set_data_types = FALSE)

title: Add metadata attributes to a data frame — add_metadata
text: data data frame containing raw dataset. metadata data frame serves metadata dictionary. must contain least columns: variable_name, label, type. Optionally, may include valueset column categorical variables, list column data frames containing value label columns. ... Additional arguments (currently unused). set_data_types Logical; TRUE, attempts coerce column data types match implied metadata. (Note: currently fully implemented.)

title: Add metadata attributes to a data frame — add_metadata
text: tibble data data, added attributes: Variable labels (via label attribute) Value labels (haven::labelled class, applicable)

title: Add metadata attributes to a data frame — add_metadata
text: function first checks structure metadata using internal helper. , variable listed metadata, : Adds label using label attribute Converts values labelled vectors using haven::labelled() valueset provided value labels present, function tries align data types data valueset (e.g., converting character codes integers necessary).

title: Add metadata attributes to a data frame — add_metadata
code: data <- data.frame(   sex = c(1, 2, 1),   age = c(23, 45, 34) )  metadata <- data.frame(   variable_name = c("sex", "age"),   label = c("Gender", "Age in years"),   type = c("categorical", "numeric"),   valueset = I(list(     data.frame(value = c(1, 2), label = c("Male", "Female")),     NULL   )) )  labelled_data <- add_metadata(data, metadata) str(labelled_data) #> 'data.frame':	3 obs. of  2 variables: #>  $ sex: dbl+lbl [1:3] 1, 2, 1 #>    ..@ labels: Named num  1 2 #>    .. ..- attr(*, "names")= chr [1:2] "Male" "Female" #>    ..@ label : chr "Gender" #>  $ age: num  23 45 34 #>   ..- attr(*, "label")= chr "Age in years"

what: Collect a lazy RCDF table into a data frame — collect
title: Collect a lazy RCDF table into a data frame — collect
text: Materialises lazy rcdf_tbl_db DuckDB-backed table regular R data frame, optionally applying variable labels value labels stored table's metadata dictionary.

title: Collect a lazy RCDF table into a data frame — collect
code: collect(data, ...)  # S3 method for class 'rcdf_tbl_db' collect(data, ...)

title: Collect a lazy RCDF table into a data frame — collect
text: data lazy rcdf_tbl_db object (returned read_rcdf lazy = TRUE), object supported dplyr::collect(). ... Additional arguments passed dplyr::collect().

title: Collect a lazy RCDF table into a data frame — collect
text: tibble rows materialised. table carries metadata dictionary, variable labels value labels applied via add_metadata returning.

title: Collect a lazy RCDF table into a data frame — collect
code: dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf") rcdf_path  <- file.path(dir, "mtcars.rcdf") prv_key    <- file.path(dir, "sample-private-key-pw.pem")  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- read_rcdf(path = rcdf_path, decryption_key = prv_key,                     password = "1234", lazy = TRUE) df <- collect(result$mtcars) class(df)  # "tbl_df" } # }

title: Decrypt string using RSA — decrypt_string
code: dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf") pub_key <- file.path(dir, 'sample-public-key.pem') prv_key <- file.path(dir, 'sample-private-key.pem') x <- encrypt_string('hello', pub_key) decrypt_string(x, prv_key = prv_key, password = '1234') #> [1] "hello"

what: Generate RSA key pair and save to files — generate_rsa_keys

code: generate_rsa_keys(path, ..., password = NULL, which = "public", prefix = NULL)

title: Generate RSA key pair and save to files — generate_rsa_keys
text: path character string specifying directory path key files .pem format saved. ... Additional arguments passed openssl::rsa_keygen() function, key size. password character string specifying password private key. NULL, private key encrypted. character string specifying key return. Can either "public" "private". Default "public". prefix character string used prefix key file names. Defaults NULL, result prefix.

title: Generate RSA key pair and save to files — generate_rsa_keys
text: character string representing file path generated key (either public private, based argument).

title: Generate RSA key pair and save to files — generate_rsa_keys
code: # Generate both public and private RSA keys and save them to the temp directory path_to <- tempdir() generate_rsa_keys(path = path_to, password = "securepassword") #> [1] "/tmp/Rtmp1PiWDF/public-key.pem"

what: Read all top-level metadata from an RCDF file — get_attrs
title: Read all top-level metadata from an RCDF file — get_attrs
text: Extracts returns complete metadata JSON stored inside .rcdf archive without decrypting loading underlying data. Useful inspecting provenance, checksums, encryption parameters, embedded data dictionaries without decryption key.

title: Read all top-level metadata from an RCDF file — get_attrs
code: get_attrs(path)

title: Read all top-level metadata from an RCDF file — get_attrs
text: path Character string. Path valid .rcdf file.

title: Read all top-level metadata from an RCDF file — get_attrs
text: named list corresponding metadata.json stored inside archive. Common keys include log_id, created_at, version, checksum, dictionary, key.

title: Read all top-level metadata from an RCDF file — get_attrs
code: dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf") rcdf_path <- file.path(dir, "mtcars.rcdf")  meta <- get_attrs(rcdf_path) meta$version #> [1] "0.1.3" meta$created_at #> [1] "2026-02-13 01:34:23"

title: Merge multiple RCDF files — merge_rcdf
text: Merge multiple RCDF files

title: Merge multiple RCDF files — merge_rcdf
code: merge_rcdf(   rcdf_files,   decryption_keys,   passwords,   merged_file_path,   pub_key = NULL )

title: Merge multiple RCDF files — merge_rcdf
text: rcdf_files character vector RCDF file paths decryption_keys Decryption keys associated RCDF file. Must match length vector passed rcdf_files argument. passwords Password associated decryption keys. Must match length decryption_keys. merged_file_path File path name merged RCDF file. pub_key Public key encrypt merged file. NULL, new RSA key pair generated.

title: Merge multiple RCDF files — merge_rcdf
text: NULL (void)

title: Merge multiple RCDF files — merge_rcdf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf")  rcdf_path <- file.path(dir, 'mtcars.rcdf') private_key <- file.path(dir, 'sample-private-key-pw.pem') pw <- '1234'  temp_dir <- tempdir()  merge_rcdf(   rcdf_files = rcdf_path,   decryption_keys = private_key,   passwords = pw,   merged_file_path = file.path(temp_dir, "merged.rcdf"),   pub_key = file.path(dir, 'sample-public-key-pw.pem') )  unlink(file.path(temp_dir, "merged.rcdf"), force = TRUE) } # }

what: rcdf: A Comprehensive Toolkit for Working with Encrypted Parquet Files — rcdf-package
title: rcdf: A Comprehensive Toolkit for Working with Encrypted Parquet Files — rcdf-package
text: Utilities reading, writing, managing RCDF files, including encryption decryption support. offers flexible interface handling data stored encrypted Parquet format, along metadata extraction, key management, secure operations using AES RSA encryptions.

title: rcdf: A Comprehensive Toolkit for Working with Encrypted Parquet Files — rcdf-package
text: Maintainer: Bhas Abdulsamad aeabdulsamad@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Read Parquet file with optional decryption — read_parquet
title: Read Parquet file with optional decryption — read_parquet
text: function reads Parquet file, optionally decrypting using provided decryption key. decryption key provided, reads file normally without decryption. supports reading Parquet files Arrow tables regular data frames, depending as_arrow_table argument.

title: Read Parquet file with optional decryption — read_parquet
code: read_parquet(   path,   ...,   decryption_key = NULL,   as_arrow_table = FALSE,   metadata = NULL )

title: Read Parquet file with optional decryption — read_parquet
text: path file path Parquet file. ... Additional arguments passed arrow::open_dataset() decryption key provided. decryption_key list containing aes_key aes_iv. provided, Parquet file decrypted using keys. Default NULL. as_arrow_table Logical. TRUE, function return result Arrow table. FALSE, regular data frame returned. Default FALSE. metadata Optional metadata (e.g., data dictionary) applied resulting data.

title: Read Parquet file with optional decryption — read_parquet
text: Arrow table data frame, depending value as_arrow_table.

title: Read Parquet file with optional decryption — read_parquet
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Using sample Parquet files from `mtcars` dataset dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf")  # Not encrypted read_parquet(file.path(dir, "mtcars.parquet"))  # Encrypted read_parquet(   file.path(dir, "mtcars-encrypted.parquet"),   decryption_key = 'rppqM5CuEqotys4wQq/g7xh6wpIjRozcAIbI9sagwKE=' ) } # }

NULL, file name used table name. columns character vector matching column names available Parquet file.

title: Read Parquet file as database — read_parquet_tbl

what: Read and decrypt RCDF data — read_rcdf
title: Read and decrypt RCDF data — read_rcdf
text: function reads RCDF file, decrypts contents using specified decryption key, loads R RCDF object.

title: Read and decrypt RCDF data — read_rcdf
code: read_rcdf(   path,   ...,   decryption_key,   password = NULL,   metadata = list(),   ignore_duplicates = TRUE,   recursive = FALSE,   return_meta = FALSE,   lazy = FALSE,   n_threads = NULL )

title: Read and decrypt RCDF data — read_rcdf
text: path string specifying path RCDF archive (zip file). directory provided, .rcdf files within directory processed. ... Additional parameters passed functions, needed (yet implemented). decryption_key key used decrypt RCDF. can RSA AES key, depending RCDF encrypted. password password used RSA decryption (optional). metadata optional list metadata object containing data dictionaries, value sets, primary key constraints data integrity measure (data.frame tibble includes least two columns: file pk_field_name. metadata applied data provided. ignore_duplicates logical flag. TRUE, warning issued duplicates found, based primary key/s defined creation RCDF file. FALSE, function stops error. recursive Logical. TRUE path directory, function search recursively .rcdf files. return_meta Logical. TRUE, metadata returned attribute RCDF object. lazy Logical. TRUE, dataset returned lazy rcdf_tbl_db DuckDB-backed table instead collected memory. underlying DuckDB connection kept alive; call collect() element ready materialise. Default FALSE. n_threads Integer NULL. Number DuckDB threads use reading. NULL (default) lets DuckDB choose based available cores.

title: Read and decrypt RCDF data — read_rcdf
text: RCDF object, list Parquet files (one record) along attached metadata.

title: Read and decrypt RCDF data — read_rcdf
code: dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf") rcdf_path <- file.path(dir, 'mtcars.rcdf') private_key <- file.path(dir, 'sample-private-key-pw.pem') pw <- '1234'  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rcdf_data <- read_rcdf(   path = rcdf_path,   decryption_key = private_key,   password = pw )  rcdf_data } # }

what: Write Parquet file with optional encryption — write_parquet
title: Write Parquet file with optional encryption — write_parquet
text: Writes data frame Parquet file. encryption_key supplied file encrypted AES using DuckDB's native Parquet encryption support. Without key file written arrow::write_parquet() supports compression codec understood Arrow library.

title: Write Parquet file with optional encryption — write_parquet
code: write_parquet(   data,   path,   ...,   encryption_key = NULL,   conn = NULL,   compression = "zstd" )

title: Write Parquet file with optional encryption — write_parquet
text: data data frame tibble write. path Character string. Destination file path Parquet file. ... Additional arguments passed arrow::write_parquet() encryption_key NULL. encryption_key raw AES key hex string (32, 48, 64 hex characters) base-64–encoded 256-bit key string. NULL (default) encryption applied. conn optional existing DuckDB DBIConnection. supplied, connection reused instead opening new one, avoids per-call setup overhead writing many tables batch. caller responsible disconnecting connection. Default NULL. compression Compression codec use unencrypted Parquet files. codec supported arrow::write_parquet() valid (e.g. "zstd", "snappy", "gzip", "lz4", "uncompressed"). Ignored encryption_key set (DuckDB chooses codec). Default "zstd".

title: Write Parquet file with optional encryption — write_parquet
text: NULL invisibly. Parquet file written path.

title: Write Parquet file with optional encryption — write_parquet
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data <- mtcars key <- "rppqM5CuEqotys4wQq/g7xh6wpIjRozcAIbI9sagwKE="  temp_dir <- tempdir()  # Encrypted write rcdf::write_parquet(   data = data,   path = file.path(temp_dir, "mtcars.parquet"),   encryption_key = key )  # Unencrypted write with gzip compression rcdf::write_parquet(   data = data,   path = file.path(temp_dir, "mtcars-gz.parquet"),   compression = "gzip" ) } # }

what: Write data to RCDF format — write_rcdf
title: Write data to RCDF format — write_rcdf
text: function writes data RCDF (Reusable Data Container Format) archive. encrypts data using AES, generates metadata, creates zip archive containing encrypted Parquet files metadata. function supports inclusion metadata system information encryption keys.

title: Write data to RCDF format — write_rcdf
code: write_rcdf(   data,   path,   pub_key,   ...,   metadata = list(),   ignore_duplicates = TRUE )

title: Write data to RCDF format — write_rcdf
text: data list data frames tables written RCDF format. element list represents record. path path RCDF file written. file saved .rcdf extension already specified. pub_key public RSA key used encrypt AES encryption keys. ... Additional arguments passed helper functions needed. metadata list metadata included RCDF file. ignore_duplicates logical flag. TRUE, warning issued duplicates found. FALSE, function stops error.

title: Write data to RCDF format — write_rcdf
text: NULL. function writes data .rcdf file specified path.

title: Write data to RCDF format — write_rcdf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example usage of writing an RCDF file  rcdf_data <- rcdf_list() rcdf_data$mtcars <- mtcars  dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf")  temp_dir <- tempdir()  write_rcdf(   data = rcdf_data,   path = file.path(temp_dir, "mtcars.rcdf"),   pub_key = file.path(dir, 'sample-public-key.pem') )  write_rcdf(   data = rcdf_data,   path = file.path(temp_dir, "mtcars-pw.rcdf"),   pub_key = file.path(dir, 'sample-public-key-pw.pem') )  unlink(file.path(temp_dir, "mtcars.rcdf"), force = TRUE) unlink(file.path(temp_dir, "mtcars-pw.rcdf"), force = TRUE) } # }

what: Write RCDF data to multiple formats — write_rcdf_as
title: Write RCDF data to multiple formats — write_rcdf_as
text: Exports RCDF-formatted data one supported open data formats. function automatically dispatches appropriate writer function based formats provided.

title: Write RCDF data to multiple formats — write_rcdf_as
code: write_rcdf_as(data, path, formats, ...)

title: Write RCDF data to multiple formats — write_rcdf_as
text: data named list RCDF object. element table tibble-like object (typically dbplyr dplyr table). path target directory output files saved. formats character vector file formats export . Supported formats include: "csv", "tsv", "json", "parquet", "xlsx", "dta", "sav", "sqlite". ... Additional arguments passed respective writer functions.

title: Write RCDF data to multiple formats — write_rcdf_as
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Files written disk.

title: Write RCDF data to multiple formats — write_rcdf_as
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ dir <- system.file("extdata", package = "rcdf") rcdf_path <- file.path(dir, 'mtcars.rcdf') private_key <- file.path(dir, 'sample-private-key-pw.pem')  rcdf_data <- read_rcdf(path = rcdf_path, decryption_key = private_key, password = '1234') temp_dir <- tempdir()  write_rcdf_as(data = rcdf_data, path = temp_dir, formats = c("csv", "xlsx"))  unlink(temp_dir, force = TRUE) } # }

title: rcdf 0.1.6
text: generate_pw(): switched Mersenne Twister cryptographically secure RNG (openssl::rand_bytes). write_parquet(): DuckDB errors longer leak raw encryption key material messages. write_rcdf_parquet(): plaintext temp table now always removed shared-connection write. write_rcdf_as(): replaced eval(parse()) dispatch safe named-list function references. hex_to_raw(): added strict input validation (type, length, character set).

title: rcdf 0.1.6
text: write_parquet() / write_rcdf_parquet(): removed httpfs overhead; shared DuckDB connection across writes; default compression changed zstd. read_rcdf(): new lazy = TRUE option DuckDB-backed lazy reads; n_threads parallel /O; duplicate PRAGMA calls deduplicated per connection. add_metadata(): label assignment vectorised batch update. open_duckdb_connection(): longer forces single-threaded mode.

title: rcdf 0.1.6
text: collect.rcdf_tbl_db(): fixed infinite-dispatch recursion; NULL metadata longer causes error. read_parquet(): unencrypted early-return path now correctly applies metadata as_arrow_table. normalize_key_value(): hex keys longer misidentified base-64.

title: rcdf 0.1.6
text: Main vignette rewritten general/non-technical users step--step workflow. Merging vignette completed fixed.

title: rcdf 0.1.5
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-07

title: rcdf 0.1.5
text: Added encrypt_value() decrypt_value() functions, respectively, encrypt decrypt string using RSA. Changed “dot env” (.env) reader read_env() read_dot_env().

title: rcdf 0.1.5
text: Fixed bugs returning metadata attributes using either read_rcdf() get_rcdf_metadata().

title: rcdf 0.1.4
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-03

title: rcdf 0.1.4
text: Added get_attr() retrieve attribute/s RCDF object. Added read_parquet_tbl() read encrypted Parquet file return lazy data DuckDB connection.

title: rcdf 0.1.4
text: Removed pre_collect argument read_rcdf()

title: rcdf 0.1.4
text: Improved SQL query string interpolation DuckDB related codes.

title: rcdf 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-12 Added merge_rcdf() function combine multiple RCDF files different encryption keys one. Updated read_rcdf() allow reading multiple RCDF file different encryption keys. Added pre_collect argument read_rcdf() allow lazy loading RCDF object.

title: rcdf 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2025-12-03 Fixed #14

title: rcdf 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-10-12 Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
